# Task Memory

> Context-preserving task management for Claude Code. Tasks remember your research across sessions.

Task Memory is an open-source tool that gives AI coding assistants persistent memory through markdown files. It automatically logs research operations, preserves context across sessions, and enforces task completion before stopping.

## Quick Start

- [README.md](README.md): Installation and usage overview
- [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md): Project-specific instructions and rules

## Core Documentation

- [docs/GETTING_STARTED.md](docs/GETTING_STARTED.md): Step-by-step setup guide
- [docs/ARCHITECTURE.md](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md): System design and data flow
- [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md): How to contribute to the project

## Skills (Claude Code Integration)

- [skills/tm-init/SKILL.md](skills/tm-init/SKILL.md): Initialize task-memory in a new project (monorepo detection, interactive setup)
- [skills/task-memory/SKILL.md](skills/task-memory/SKILL.md): Full task workflow guide
- [skills/task-status/SKILL.md](skills/task-status/SKILL.md): Quick context verification (5-Question Reboot Test)
- [skills/task-memory/MONOREPO.md](skills/task-memory/MONOREPO.md): Monorepo configuration patterns
- [skills/task-memory/TROUBLESHOOTING.md](skills/task-memory/TROUBLESHOOTING.md): Common issues and solutions

## Rules and Hooks

- [rules/task-memory.md](rules/task-memory.md): Task workflow enforcement rules
- [hooks/README.md](hooks/README.md): Hook events and configuration
- [hooks/hooks.json](hooks/hooks.json): Plugin hook configuration

## Configuration

- [.claude/settings.json](.claude/settings.json): Claude Code hook configuration
- [.claude-plugin/plugin.json](.claude-plugin/plugin.json): Plugin manifest
- [.claude-plugin/marketplace.json](.claude-plugin/marketplace.json): Marketplace listing

## Examples

- [examples/tasks.md](examples/tasks.md): Sample task board with all field types
- [examples/archive.md](examples/archive.md): Sample completed task archive
- [examples/notes/](examples/notes/): Sample research documentation

## Task Format

```markdown
### TASK-XXX | Title

**Priority**: High | **Category**: Feature | **Status**: in-progress
**Workflow**: Feature | **Complexity**: Standard
**Created**: YYYY-MM-DD | **Started**: YYYY-MM-DD | **Finished**:
**Tags**: #tag1 #tag2

Description of what needs to be done.

**Subtasks**:
- [ ] Phase 1: Setup
- [ ] Phase 2: Implement (depends: Phase 1)
- [ ] Phase 3: Test (depends: Phase 2)

**Notes**:

**Visual Operations Log**:

**Errors Log**:
```

## Key Concepts

| Concept | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| Status Field | Authoritative source for task state: `todo`, `in-progress`, `done` |
| 2-Action Rule | Save research findings after every 2 visual operations |
| Session Tracking | Hooks only block for tasks worked on in current session |
| Pre-Work Checklist | Read files, search patterns, identify gotchas before implementing |

## Installation Methods

1. **Git Clone**: `git clone https://github.com/kepptic/task-memory.git` (hooks auto-load)
2. **Copy Files**: Copy `hooks/`, `skills/`, `rules/`, `planning/`, and `.claude/settings.json` to your project
3. **Plugin Install**: `/plugin install task-memory@kepptic`
4. **Standalone**: Download `task-memory.html` and open in browser
